page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6.4xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by stress-ng single-core CPU scores and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression tests, which fall into the bottom 25% of benchmarks. However, multi-core execution scales to average levels, with stress-ng multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth is also average, reaching 780.06 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 88.67 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is structured for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for multi-threaded processing tasks. It is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, and database operations, where its average multi-core and memory throughput can be fully utilized.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of memory. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Benchmark data reveals a contrast between weak single-core performance and average multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Redis SET operations all scoring in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth is also average, reaching 88.67 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance delivers qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized compute tasks. It is best suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core performance for database and web workloads.

tagline: A dedicated 16-core Intel Xeon compute instance with a balanced 1:2 memory-to-CPU ratio for multi-threaded workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
